CROSS DEFENDANTS SWEAT EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA, TURBINEONE, LLC'S Motion To Seal In Support Of Plaintiff Cross-Defendants' Motions For Summary Adjudication

Denied without prejudice to re-filing before Judge Karnow in Department 611 (contact his clerk to secure a hearing date and time). The only bases argued in the memorandum of points and authorities (MPA) are (i) the parties have agreed the material is confidential in discovery and (ii) similar orders have been issued in the past. Neither is a reason for sealing now. There is no evidence (e.g., a declaration from a client who has firsthand knowledge) that the material is so confidential it cannot be exposed to the public. The proposed order makes no required findings. See CRC 2.550 ff. Boilerplate will not suffice. If a new motion is filed the party seeking protection should provide a "delta document" showing the specific words or numbers sought to be redacted from the public file. See https://works.bepress.com/curtis_karnow/13/

It is usually not proper to seal an entire document- often much of the data, such as a title, date, boilerplate, and other content, does not give away any secrets. None of that should be redacted.
Regardless of any agreement among the parties, the only party which should seek sealing is the party which desires sealing. See CRC 2.551 and especially 2.551(b)(3)(B).

The moving parties may also consider withdrawing items (or pages of them with sensitive data) which, on reflection, need not be part of the record for the pending summary adjudication motions.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

A proposed order is emailed to the Court for review.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR #13859, 714-471-2597, justusbalentinecsr@ggmail.com, reported.(302/CEAK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Proper Forum, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Matthew Borden, Esq., (415) 599-0210, borden@braunhagey.com, BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP, for Plaintiff, Sweat Equity Partners, LLC
George Morris, (415) 318-1200, gmorris@kslaw.com, KING & SPALDING LLP, for Defendant Swoop Search, LLC

Defendant's counsel contests the Court's tentative ruling.

Counsel, George Morris, Esq. argues this motion on behalf of defendants.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANTS QUINN BOTTUM and SWOOP SEARCH, LLC'S Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Proper Forum.

Defendants move to dismiss under CCP 410.30 solely "on the grounds that a valid and enforceable contractual forum selection clause is applicable." (Not. 2:7-10; CCP 1010.) Defendants rely on a forum selection clause in a confidentiality agreement - one of at least three contracts the parties entered. (Memo. 2:21-3:17.) However, plaintiff asserts - and review of its complaint confirms - that it is not suing under the confidentiality agreement. (Id. at 11:1-12:13, 18:20-21.) Hence, the forum selection clause does not apply, and defendants' motion is denied. None of defendants' cited California authorities holds that a forum selection clause in one contract applies to disputes under another contract. Indeed, the forum selection clause in the parties' confidentiality contract shows they knew how to include such a clause when they so intended - something they did not do in their other two agreements. I do not opine on forum-related issues defendants did not notice as grounds for their motion. (CCP 1010.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And To Stay Discovery And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-12-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHRISTMAN RICE, ESQ. of Braunhagey & Borden LLP (Tel: 646-829-9403) for plaintiff Sweat Equity Partners, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS QUINN BOTTUM and SWOOP SEARCH, LLC's Motion For Protective Order And To Stay Discovery.

Plaintiffs stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for plaintiffs appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested. Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the modification of adding a date certain, as follows:

Motion for stay DENIED. California courts have long held that discovery is still permitted even if a demurrer is sustained; indeed, sometimes such discovery will be necessary for a party to properly re-plead. Budget Finance Plan v. Sup. Ct. (1973) 34 Cal.App.3d 794, 797-98. Thus, the mere pendency of a demurrer and motion to dismiss does not preclude discovery. To the extent Defendants seek a protective order with respect to any allegedly overbroad or otherwise objectionable request, the motion is denied without prejudice for failure to establish good cause with respect to each request, CCP 2030.090, 2031.060, and for failure to include a separate statement, CRC 3.1345. Without specific briefing the Court cannot be expected to review the discovery requests and pick out which ones are entitled to protection from those that are not. To the extent any discovery requests are "duplicative" or "overbroad" the parties should meet and confer about them and, if necessary, file targeted motions to compel or for protective orders. Any such efforts are likely to be relevant to both this California state action and the federal action. August 27, 2021 set as the date certain to produce.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Complaint And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. GEORGE MORRIS, Esq. of King & Spalding LLP (Tel: 415-318-1243) for defendants Swoop Search, LLC and Quinn Bottum;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SWOOP SEARCH, LLC's Motion To Seal Portions Of Complaint.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Swoop Search, LLC's motion to seal portions of the complaint is granted. The court finds facts to establish: (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest." (California Rules of Court, rule 2.550(d).)

Swoop Search submits the declaration of Paul Bottum, CEO. Bottum states Swoop develops proprietary products used by government and military organizations to ensure secure communications and data acquisition. (Bottum Decl., 5.) Swoop has a cleared government Top Secret facility and takes extensive measures to protect confidential information. (Id. 6.) Disclosing sensitive business information to the general public would irrefutably cause Swoop competitive harm. (Id. 7, 9.) The information to be sealed includes proprietary products, trade secrets, confidential financial information, and private company valuations. (Id. 6.)

The protection of trade secrets in an interest that can support sealing records in a civil proceeding. (McGuan v. Endovascular Technologies, Inc. (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 974, 988 ["'Trade secret' means information, including a formula, pattern, compilation, program, device, method, technique, or process, that: (1) Derives independent economic value, actual or potential from not being generally known to the public or to other persons who can obtain economic value from its disclosure or use; and (2) Is the subject of efforts that are reasonable under the circumstances to maintain its secrecy."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
